title: Request Headers
overview: Set and validate custom and standard HTTP headers.
whyItMatters: Headers control metadata like content types, client identifiers, cache controls, and API versioning. Ensuring headers are sent and received correctly is crucial for robust API communication.
explanation: You can add headers to a request using the `header("Name", "Value")` method in the `given()` block. Common headers like `Content-Type` and `Accept` have dedicated methods (`contentType()` and `accept()`). To validate headers returned by the server, use `header("Name", expectedValue)` or `headers(...)` in the `then()` block.
keyPoints: Use dedicated `.contentType()` and `.accept()` for standard MIME type headers.
Pass multiple headers at once using the `Headers` class or a Map.
codeExample:
given().
    baseUri("https://api.example.com").
    header("X-Request-ID", "req-999").
    header("X-API-Version", "v2").
    accept(ContentType.JSON).
when().
    get("/data").
then().
    statusCode(200).
    header("X-Rate-Limit-Remaining", notNullValue()).
    header("Content-Type", containsString("application/json"));
realWorldExample: APIs often require an API key in a custom header like `x-api-key`. You pass this using `.header("x-api-key", "secret-key")`. On the response, you might validate that rate-limiting headers like `X-RateLimit-Remaining` are present.
commonMistakes: Misspelling header keys, which the server ignores, leading to default behavior rather than failures.
Asserting exact equality on `Content-Type` when it includes a charset (e.g., `application/json; charset=utf-8`), causing test failures. Use `containsString()` instead.
Not sending the `Accept` header when the API supports multiple formats (JSON, XML).
bestPractices: Use global `RequestSpecification` to add common headers (like API keys or User-Agents) to all requests.
Validate important response headers like CORS headers (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin`) if testing a public API.
Use `containsString` for `Content-Type` validation to avoid charset mismatch.

interviewQuestions.q: How do you validate a specific header in the response?
interviewQuestions.a: You use `.then().header("Header-Name", "Expected Value")`. You can also use Hamcrest matchers for partial validation.

interviewQuestions.q: What is the difference between Content-Type and Accept headers?
interviewQuestions.a: Content-Type describes the format of the data being sent in the request body. Accept describes the format the client wants to receive in the response body.

revisionSummary: Use .header() in given() to send headers, and .header() in then() to validate them.
